Up (2009) Simply put, it's incredible. I can say with great certainty that Pixar is the only studio that can create amazing animations with real and true themes.
Good. A bit overrated, but overall good.
It’s not just a balloon-lifted house — it’s a ship of love and fantasy floating in the sky

I realize this is generally a controversial take, but I believe Up to be a fairly overrated Pixar film. But I mean, it’s still great. Very charming, has all of the Pixar magic, and can be devastatingly emotional, especially that first 10 minutes. But, I have always found it to be a bit slower after the opening montage, and not really as consistently entertaining as most of the other Pixar flicks at that point. But like I said, it’s still a very good film.
The best Pixar film

Different pieces of a plot used together, making something great. I believe the goodness & emotion in the storyline. Throughout her life & marriage, Ellie dreamed of going to Paradise Falls but those dreams get snuffed due to her death. I got to also feel bad for Carl (the widower) whose loved never got to have her dream come true. If Carl still referred to Ellie, does that mean he can see ghosts? Carl was selfless & was committed to things like bring his house UP & away with him so he did not have to lose it to a retirement home being in construction. He was also committed to being selfless & helping others like the scout who is Russel who got lost & Carl made sure he was safe & with him along the way, his heart changed while being with Russel.*¹ CARL DOES IT FOR HIS WIFE Other characters like him were loveable, especially the optimistic dog who was Dug being clever as he could sniff the way like any dog does. There was also the stubborn dog, Alpha, with the high pitched voice making him funny & Russel was right about his voice. Charles Muntz is a very interesting character being connected to the quotes "never meet your hero in person" & "you either die a hero or live long enough to become a villain". This is because he inspired Fredrickson to go on an adventure to Paradise Falls yet he turned out to be the antagonist with an army of dogs which Dug was originally a part of.*⅔ Those elements I pointed out are that it's plot combined of storyline to these movies that are ¹"Ice Age", ²"Turbo" & ³"How to Train Your Dragon 2" Many Pixar movies are heartfelt.
This movie will always be in my heart. I love sad films that balance the sadness and this does that perfectly.
Working at a school and watching this movie there's alot of similarities in the youth wanting to achieve and fit in. Loved the connection beaten the grumpy old man and the young eager kid.

La séquence "A Married Life" en a sûrement fait pleuré plus d'un. Simplement l'histoire d'une promesse tenue qui fait monter les larmes aux yeux. Le film est rempli d'émotions, a son lot d'action et suit la renaissance de Carl qui semblait s'être éteinte après le départ d'Ellie. L'aventure vécue aux côtés de Russell et de Doug permet à Carl de réaliser qu'il y a encore de belles choses à vivre même si l'amour de sa vie n'est plus à ses côtés... la vie continue.

I should have watched this film much sooner. It certainly lives "up" to its reputation as a fun adventure, despite its rather emotional beginning. It is one of the best animated portrayals of dealing with grief, especially with its extremely relatable protagonist, Carl.

I love it 7: the comedy is actually amazing?!!?!?? 8: it shows great character growth, at the beginning Carl won’t let anything happen to his house, but at the end he says “it’s just a house,I can get another” 9: voice acting is peak 10: latso from Toy Story 3 is in this movie
